additive_correction.description: User asks for something the model should have included on the first pass. Strong signal of a missing default behavior.

verification_request.description: User asks if the model checked something it should have checked unprompted. Suggests a missing verification step.

still_wrong.description: User signals the result is still incorrect after one or more attempts. Suggests a fragile/recurring failure mode.

stop_doing.description: User tells the model to stop a behavior. Strong direct prohibition signal.

actually_correction.description: User corrects a model assumption. Often indicates the model leaped to a conclusion that should have been verified.

wrong_target.description: User points out the model edited/read/operated on the wrong file or scope. Suggests bad target selection heuristics.

explicit_instruction.description: User explicitly states a rule, often after the model violated it. These are the highest-yield rule candidates.
